diagrams.title: Reduction Execution Flow
diagrams.description: Shows how reduction works across multiple threads with private copies and final combination
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how OpenMP creates private copies, performs operations, and combines results at the end of parallel region. The diagram shows 4 threads each accumulating values into their private copy, then all copies combined using the reduction operator (+ in this case) to produce final result.

diagrams.title: Reduction Identity Values Table
diagrams.description: Table showing identity values for all supported reduction operators
diagrams.purpose: Quick reference for remembering identity values. The identity value is what each thread's private copy starts with before performing reduction operations. Understanding these is crucial for correct reduction behavior.
